name: Larry Adler
abstract: Lawrence Cecil Adler (February 10, 1914 – August 6, 2001) was an American harmonica player and film composer. Known for playing major works, he played compositions by George Gershwin, Ralph Vaughan Williams, Malcolm Arnold, Darius Milhaud and Arthur Benjamin.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: they'd have people like Larry Adler, who is my biggest nightmare because he was playing all these “Rhapsody in Blue” and Ravel’s “Bolero” and I couldn’t read note one. So I was faking everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: Peter Lind Hayes and Larry Adler, the harmonica player’s brother Jerry Adler played harmonica... we wound up playing shows on aircraft carriers and all through the Mariana Islands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: what's this harmonica player, Larry Adler and he can hear Little Walter.
